text: Deadlines are a common part of work and study, but handling them well requires a few key skills. First, use time management by breaking large tasks into smaller steps and planning ahead. Second, prioritization helps you focus on what is most important or urgent, so you do not waste time on less critical work. Scope management is also crucial: avoid adding extra tasks or details that are not necessary, as this can delay your progress. Finally, communication is essential; if you foresee a problem, tell your team or manager early. By staying organized and realistic, you can meet deadlines with less stress.
